Earthcaller  
 

Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY
Skill: 1H Slashing Atk Delay: 24
DMG: 14 Dmg Bonus: 35
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STR: +15 DEX: +10 STA: +5 HP: +50
SV FIRE: +5 SV DISEASE: +5 SV COLD: +5 SV MAGIC: +5 SV POISON: +5
Effect: Earthcall (Combat, Casting Time: Instant) at Level 50
WT: 2.0 Size: MEDIUM
Class: RNG
Race: HUM ELF HEF HFL DRK
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)
Earthcaller
Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY
Skill: 1H Slashing Atk Delay: 24
DMG: 14 Dmg Bonus: 35
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STR: +15 DEX: +10 STA: +5 HP: +50
SV FIRE: +5 SV DISEASE: +5 SV COLD: +5 SV MAGIC: +5 SV POISON: +5
Effect: Earthcall (Combat, Casting Time: Instant) at Level 50
WT: 2.0 Size: MEDIUM
Class: RNG
Race: HUM ELF HEF HFL DRK
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Item Lore:Blessed by the Earth Goddess
Item Type:1H Slashing
Light:Stein of Tears
Stackable:No
Lucy Entry By:unknown
Item Updated By:SoD-AB Deillusional
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2022-11-23 18:55:15
Page Updated:Sun Dec 15th, 2019

Expansion: Ruins of Kunark
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 50
[Quests | Recipes | Comments ]

This item is the result of a quest.
Expansion List - Premium only.
Quest Name
Ranger Epic: Swiftwind and Earthcaller

Quests

This item is used in quests.
Expansion List - Premium only.

East Karana
Quest Name
Ranger Epic 1.5: Heartwood Blade

Nedaria's Landing
Quest Name
Ranger Epic 1.5 Pre-Quest
Ranger Epic 2.0: Aurora, the Heartwood Blade

Northern Felwithe
Quest Name
An Epic Request (Ranger)
Used in 1 recipe.
Recipe list - Premium only.

Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
North Karana
One of two results to the Ranger Particle Weapon Quest. The other is Swiftwind.


Uploaded December 15th, 2019 by iventheassassin
Send a correction
« Previous 1 2 Next »
Post Comment
Slow mitigated with more recent mobs/expansions
QuoteReply
# Mar 06 2009 at 7:38 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1686529028957
Scholar
342 posts
All slow effects (including the RNG epic proc) are mitigated more and more with newer expansions. You will see messages like "...was mostly slowed" or "...was partially slowed".

What is nice about this weapon's effect (and the Wavecrasher's) is they add a 20 DoT component. The benefit here is that you can SEE if the slow is on a mob and often which one, unless you have multiple of the same mob name. Very useful for those groups with out the "Inspect Buffs" Leadership AA benefit.
▲ Page top
when the shamans and chanters are scarce
QuoteReply
# Dec 24 2006 at 11:54 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591813790348
6 posts
This is excellent for moments when a slower isnt around.
Yes it produces aggro , but by using jolting blades and or jolt , this isnt much of a problem. Just melle till it procs , jolt and back off and shoot arrows. The mob is slowed and you're still fullfilling your role in the group , and then some. 50% slow is way better than no slow.

Vermyllion Daggerthorn of Drinal Ranger of the 67th Equinox
▲ Page top
Finally got the SEoC
QuoteReply
# Oct 06 2004 at 10:50 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1660522686317
Scholar
166 posts
Just want to post some of my personal experience with the shattered emerald of corruption drop for this fine weapon. I spent quite awhile in Plane of Hate trying to get this thing to drop. In my time there I managed to kill almost all the named mobs that are accesable from the stairs. I had just about every epic piece that drops in hate drop, with the exception of the SEoC until last night. I found that the best chance for getting this piece is from either Magi P`Tasa at spawn point # 1 on the PoH map and from an eerie chest at spawn point # 11 on the same map. The only named mobs in Teir 1 that I did not kill when I was in Hate were Coercer T`Vala #10, Avatar of Adhorrence #9 and a hidiouse rat #12. They were just to far out from the staris to pull with my small groups.

I only saw the SEoC drop from the chest and Magi, but only was able to loot the item last night since I was the only ranger and did not need to rely on the RNG to win the item. Saw it twice off both of those in all my times in hate. So, I am guessing that these are the best chances to get this drop, just in case any rangers are suffering through hate as I did.

My groups usually consited of me as the tank, lvl 53 ranger, an enchanter (could usually charm a hate mob to help me with DPS), and either a shaman or a druid. Only died once due to Lag/LD and never had to many problems with this set up. It was slow at times but the mobs can be slowed and nuked. Good luck to the rangers of Norath. This is the map.

http://eq.chedrock.com/Hate_b_Tier1.gif

▲ Page top
haste %
QuoteReply
# Sep 13 2004 at 5:56 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592761406864
20 posts
it would be nice to find out the EXACT % of slow on this. to bad gm's wont tell us.
▲ Page top
RE: haste %
QuoteReply
# Oct 20 2004 at 7:09 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592260151178
119 posts

They don't have to, a lot of spell info can be read from the eq files, given the right software. This is a 50% slow.
▲ Page top
effect damage re-tweaks
QuoteReply
# Jan 27 2004 at 3:28 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592759869422
6 posts
Since swiftwind haste,is listed as a proc, then I would hope it would stack with other haste spells and items, but after reading the post here i dought it. As for the slow rate of the earthcaller 50% seems ok and 20 points/tick is not too bad. But I hope that after all the trouble that you must go through and all the time it takes to reserch the raids (3 if you buy the jade reaver in bazaar 4 if not) It would be nice to see the damage increase up 2 points per weapon and a 40/tick (20 point increase) with it stacking with other haste items other than spells which i was told it dose. EQ dose need to re-tweak its older quests and make up some new ones as part of there new expansions, but I'm not sure if its profitable enough to do so.
▲ Page top
effect damage re-tweaks
QuoteReply
# Jan 18 2008 at 7:35 PM Rating: Decent
dafdaf
View User Forum
Scholar
1,273 posts
4 years on from this post, and this is still one item that any ranger can have and wield it proudly in the knowledge that even the very best rangers on the server have it saved, ready to use in their bandolier.

It's one of the most useful epic 1.0s, and I suspect it will be a long time after I stop using my epic 2.0 that I will retire my earthcaller.

98 posts
Swiftspirit is 40% worn haste.

Hmmmmm!
QuoteReply
# Jan 03 2004 at 12:54 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592257086902
6 posts
Can a lvl 51 ranger do epic with help of 65 mage and 65 shaman. Or would i need more people? Plz send me a tell.
▲ Page top
RE: Hmmmmm!
QuoteReply
# Feb 01 2004 at 12:48 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591795405054
10 posts
You can do most of it solo since its gather and hand in, the first fight in E.Karana u can do with your 2 friends, maybe the hole fight also, but take a group jsut in case... the KC fight you can do with a good group (jsut for clairfication, imo, a good group is LDoN Hard type group) so yeah... 2 of the fights are doable with your 2 friends but bringing more is always a good thing =)

▲ Page top
RE: Hmmmmm!
QuoteReply
# Mar 09 2004 at 1:18 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591787269702
87 posts
Laura is bang on with her comments given the level of your friends and that having a full grp is best for the Hole. Getting the SSoC from PoAir is of similar difficulty and a solid group can get this easily. You don't actually have to go to PoAir as this item can be MQ'd. If your guild happens to go there, let them know as I am sure they would be more than happy help you get 40% worn haste with an off-hand weapon that is decent.

Unfortunately Earthcaller is not as big a boon as Swiftwind since I am currently using Bloodstained Axe of the Catacombs in primary 15/21 vs 14/24 for Earthcaller. If you are young enough when you get it, it may be of benefit especially when soloing, but at the high end, MObs tend to resist being slowed and such so a faster higher DMG weapon is a better trade than the proc.
▲ Page top
Slow %
QuoteReply
# Dec 12 2003 at 10:41 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592255003256
15 posts
Alakhazam says its a 50pct slow. Everlore says its a 70pct slow. Anyone actually know wich is right? Plz post if you actually know from exp, not if you have read it somewere else, and if so explain your reasoning.

I have been using swarm caller when i solo, and i think that is also a 50pct slow. So the only real advantage would be the higher chance of a proc since you can duel weild this with swift, But dam i realy do wish this is a 70pct slow
▲ Page top
RE: Slow %
QuoteReply
# Mar 23 2004 at 5:45 PM Rating: Default
Aquendar
View User Forum
Scholar
195 posts
But the Swarmcaller proc isn't also a 500hp DoT over two and a half minutes. So, swing Earthcaller until in procs, then switch back to Stained Axe of the Scions or better! Nice lingering effect to help your DPS and significantly cut the mob's.
____________________________
Farwarden Aquendar Lerilon, 105 Season Human Ranger of Tunare

Was 70% until they nerfed it. Now it's 50%.

RE: Slow %
QuoteReply
# Dec 12 2003 at 10:48 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592255003256
15 posts
Oh i just looked up swarmcaller it is a 50pct slow and last 3.3 min instead of the earthcaller's 2.5 That means that every 2min i have to swich back earthcaller to get a proc if battle last to long. I realy hope someone reads this post. I would love it if this thing turns out to be a 70pct slow
▲ Page top
RE: Slow %
QuoteReply
# Feb 23 2006 at 6:20 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592770347324
View User Forum
10 posts
earthcaller is only 50% now but I would swap it in instead of swarmcaller anyday, consider the 500 DoT and the fact that you can dual wield whatever your secondary is for added damage. not to mention the ratio. earthy procs relatively quick and at least your DPS doesn't go in the dumpster waitg for it.
▲ Page top
Did it at 46
QuoteReply
# Sep 02 2003 at 2:45 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591754955647
Scholar
88 posts
He wouldn't speak to me at 44, so I used my 65 druid to trigger the quest and do up to the EK reavers. Then had my ranger loot the tomb and continue.

I waited until 46 to hand the sword in to Telin to get swifty just in case as it was a lot of work and easier just to level up a bit. It wouldn't surprise me you could do this at level 1 with the right faction and someone else to trigger the first part.

QuoteReply
# Jul 01 2003 at 9:41 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592251985559
Scholar
30 posts
Greetings all... just to inform you... VI is not in charge of EQ any more and hasnt been for sometime now... SOE is calling the ball in the game now... now as far as to what lvl is the lowest you can start this epic... it was lvl 46 when my ranger Truueshott was able to start his... I tried at lvls 44 and 45... but 46 was when he would talk to me... that is all I have to say... May Tunare protect you

Edited, Tue Jul 1 09:59:24 2003

Huh?
QuoteReply
# Feb 24 2003 at 3:54 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591786535718
37 posts
I just read further down that someone had sucessfully talked to Telin at 35. I am 36 and he considers me kindly, and he told me that I was too inexperienced. I would like to know what the genuine minumum level to start and finish both halfs of the epic. Also, I am quite bummed that the epics are falling far behind the rest of the game, and was wondering if anyone knows if Verant is planning new epics. Also, while we're on the subject, what are the best weapons for a ranger at the top of his game?
▲ Page top
RE: Huh?
QuoteReply
# Aug 25 2003 at 8:10 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1644797901812
Scholar
39 posts
Just another quick confirmation that you do need to be at least lvl 46 for Telin to give you the note. I went to speak to him at 45 (with ally faction) and was also told that I was too inexperienced, however, at 46 I returned and was successful in obtaining the note to kick off my epic. If he doesn't appear to speak with you at max faction, try saying "What actions", even if you aren't prompted. That's how I triggered the trade to get the note. Good luck, fellow Rangers!
▲ Page top
RE: Huh?
QuoteReply
# Jun 06 2003 at 6:57 PM Rating: Excellent
Aquendar
View User Forum
Scholar
195 posts
You have to be at least 46 to talk to Telin, but a lot of the other stuff can be done once foraging breaks 100 (101 +). You can't do final turn in until 50 or Faelin eats it, so keep that in mind.
____________________________
Farwarden Aquendar Lerilon, 105 Season Human Ranger of Tunare

pop upgrade?
QuoteReply
# Jan 12 2003 at 10:59 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592759368833
70 posts
with the new pop armor and stuff that makes kael and SS armor look like newbie gear, there are starting to be mroe and more weapons that just make epics look like garbage.i understand the illusion of this weapon with the huge slow on it, but really look at it, by the time ur high enough to get this weapon u will be grouping, when ur goruping in any high lvl place a slower is needed, and trying to rely on a weapon proc for main sourse of slowing. so what it comes down to is ratio. i would really like to see some epic quest add on, where u could some how continue the quest leading up to an upgrade for the weapons.
▲ Page top
Minor Corrections
QuoteReply
# Aug 24 2002 at 9:09 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592255540052
2 posts
I suppose this has been modified by Verant since these posts, but a couple of corrections:

1) You can forage the items prior to Skill Level (100). I did it at around skill (65)

2) You can talk to the NPC's prior to Level 50. I did it at 46.

I need the Jade Reaver, The Hole, and PoA to complete the first, plus the stone from hate for the second. PoA we are doing in a week, the Hole we can do anytime. I can't wait, its been forever it seems. Now that I think about it I started on it 6 months ago.
▲ Page top

QuoteReply

__DEL__1592253966268

#Zaztik, Posted: Feb 18 2002 at 7:06 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) FULL Ranger Epic Weapon quest walkthrough!!!!
Level 50?
QuoteReply
# Jan 30 2002 at 3:57 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591750558205
36 posts
I hear that you need to be level 50+ to finish the quest, yet you need to be at least 47 To get to the Plane of Air. I'm almost done, I need to raid VS, The Hole, PoA and PoH. Can I finish the entire quest and have my epics at 47?
▲ Page top
RE: Level 50?
QuoteReply
# Jul 01 2002 at 6:17 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591770260749
Scholar
104 posts
Pardon me for saying so, but isn't that a little more than "almost done"? You have some planes raids ahead of you for openers. Most of the other stuff is just foraging and turn-ins.

Safe Travels
▲ Page top
RE: Level 50? Nope.
QuoteReply
# Jun 27 2002 at 2:33 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593361580475
24 posts
It's been done prior to level 50: by below planes-level characters, in fact.

Originally, VI said that the epic quests were intended for 50+ players. Some of the epic quests have a level requirement, but the ranger epic does not - it's driven by faction only.

That said, I'd be very careful about the final turn-ins if my PC was < 50. You never know when VI will "fix" the quest and not tell anyone.
▲ Page top
RE: Level 50?
QuoteReply
# Feb 11 2002 at 7:38 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591752579117
18 posts
"I need to raid VS, The Hole, PoA and PoH"

Doesn't sound like you're almost done to me, heh. The Hate piece is rare as hell too. If you CAN get all the items, you should be able to complete the quest. Earthcaller may not proc til 51 though.
▲ Page top
RE: Level 50?
QuoteReply
# May 11 2003 at 11:46 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592253812287
74 posts
i saw a lvl48 Ranger with both his epics, only problem he said was that the effect and proc dont work for him. So, im assuming that you can GET the epics prior to lvl 51, but the effect and the proc wont take effect until lvl 51.
▲ Page top
DOT AoE or Single Mob ?
QuoteReply
# Jan 10 2002 at 4:28 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591769972526
2 posts
Is the DOT proc on this weapon an AoE (Area of Effect) or a single mob DOT spell ?

I hope it isn't AoE cause that would be breaking mezzes left and right.
▲ Page top
RE: DOT AoE or Single Mob ?
QuoteReply
# Feb 12 2002 at 4:59 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592758252101
11 posts
The proc is not a DOT. It's 40% slow and it's single-target.

Can complete both epics in a day with a single group if all mobs are up, and you're lucky with drops. Pretty hard to do cleric, enchanter or warrior in that time without a very large and efficient force.

This is cake compared to most other epics cept maybe rouge or monk

To clear something else up...
QuoteReply
# Aug 12 2001 at 1:26 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765348689
1 post
First of all, you dont have a choice which hand your weapons go it. Swiftwind is Offhand only, while Earthcaller is Primary only.

Also, i have been told, and have read that the haste on swiftwind is 45%, not 40%. and it also gives and ATK boost, which i have read to be +30, but when i equipped the weapon, my ATK went up 53 points, so im not so sure.

As for earthcaller, again ive read and been told, the proc is 70% slow, not 50%. as well as the 500 DoT.

Ermak Swiftstrider <House Hydra>
Pathfinder of 51 Seasons
Tholuxe

To clear something else up...
QuoteReply
# Dec 18 2016 at 1:19 AM Rating: Decent

Scholar
24 posts
Yes your ark went up due to the str added. There is no ark power buff from the weapon.

# Jun 27 2002 at 2:38 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593361580475
24 posts
Haste on SW is 40%.

The slow proc on EC scales with level, maxing out at 50% at level 60.

It went up to 53 because the STR on the weapon also adds ATK :)

probably not
QuoteReply
# Jul 19 2001 at 2:19 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249543153
27 posts
nah, this is one half of the ranger epic (best weapons a ranger can wield), you usually can't even start the quest until after 45. each class has an epic weapon, wielding one usually means months of work.

at level 15, i'd just save some money and buy a decent weapon from your guild/local market (north freeport, faydark).
▲ Page top
RE: probably not
QuoteReply
# May 20 2002 at 5:31 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592253920662
Scholar
72 posts
These haven't been the best weapons since Velious came out... Starting with Primal Velium weapons (usually 13/20 with tons of stats plus an avatar proc) and their priceless counterpart (less stats and no proc but same ratio) and working all the way up to say Tolan's Longsword of the Glade... 15/21 either hand good amount of stats 40 percent haste with attack buff. Luclin brings even better weapons, but of course it wasn't out in June 2001. There still remains much to be discovered and much that has been discovered is still not know to me... but I have seen weapons as good as 13/17 and 16/20 and have heard of even better.

Uhh? 1-60 qeust..but level 50 to attain??
QuoteReply
# Jun 17 2001 at 2:14 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Hey. Sorry if this sounds dumb, but on the quest search it says,'1-60 levels'..But the weapons both say, 'Level 50 to attain.' I'm only lev 15, and wondering..can I do tis quest or not??? Please help, thanks.

Dealus
15 Ranger
▲ Page top
RE: Uhh? 1-60 qeust..but level 50 to attain??
QuoteReply
# May 20 2002 at 5:26 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592253920662
Scholar
72 posts
Parts of the epic can be done at any level, for example looting of the stone from VS (Who is a complete pushover in his reanimated form) or buying/looting a Jade Reaver (It'd be hard to loot either of those though without the whole zone being cleared out, but with some luck it is doable. You can also forage many of the items needed starting in your 20's, as soon as your forage hits 100. I have been saving that chilled tundra root(Everfrost) for about 25 levels now (I'm 51).
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jan 29 2002 at 8:25 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) BUH HA HA HAH HA HA HA HA HA HA!!!!! MUH HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA~!!!!!!! (have you read what you have to do on this quest?)
RE: Uhh? 1-60 qeust..but level 50 to attain??
QuoteReply
# Nov 19 2001 at 9:28 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591752611466
Scholar
76 posts
No, the quest which results in these 2 wonderful swords, can only be done by level 46+ rangers. Some of the pieces to each particle weapon/epic come from the Planes of Hate, Sky, or Fear. The minimum level to get into these zones to even loot the piece is level 46. Many mobs require even higher levels to take down, but if you have a strong guild to help you out or a guild with several strong allies, you can usually get the weapons a little earlier than normal.
▲ Page top
RE: Uhh? 1-60 qeust..but level 50 to attain??
QuoteReply
# Feb 12 2002 at 5:04 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592758252101
11 posts
Technically, you don't have to go to PoH or PoA for this. Both those pieces are multiquestable. PoF has no part in the epic at all. So it's conceivable that you could get all your pieces together at a lower level. I don't know if you could turn them in though.
▲ Page top
RE: Uhh? 1-60 qeust..but level 50 to attain??
QuoteReply
# May 31 2002 at 12:25 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592756575111
5 posts
I didn't start until I was past 50, but I belive the npcs will only respond to you if you are 50+.

If I see a lvl 15 ranger running around with epics im gonna retire and open up the first strip club in Ecoms.

RE: Uhh? 1-60 qeust..but level 50 to attain??
QuoteReply
# Sep 27 2002 at 5:25 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591774324540
1 post
None of these things are true. I started working on my epic at 35 when I went to speak with Telin. He will NOT talk to you if you have screwed your faction with him some. At 35 I had gotten the note from Telin, found the druid in Grater Fay, Gave the ring to the dark elf in Kith (I had sow up, shouted that I was about to spawn a reaver, handed him the ring and ran like mad for the zone)

I did every possible part I could up to the corrupter which I did at 40 with the aid of a guild that had just done it for their ranger.

So to clear things up some, yes many parts of the epic can be done well before 46.

Kulaas
52 Pathfinder
Druzzil Ro
